meaning: vi.虚张声势,冲激,溅出vt.摇晃,冲洗n.泼水声,虚张声势,吓唬
examples: Slowly, slowly. Don't swash the soup.
Set up the participant, Swish Swash, company profile.
How to reconcile the reality of this large and increasingly conservative sector with its swash-buckling and secretive image?
But I can say that at first blush, it seems AC4 looks like a decent enough seafaring game, with more than enough buckles to swash.
